title: Security
description:
The security directory is responsible for managing security-related functionality in the Hadoop MapReduce architecture. It collaborates with other files and directories to ensure the secure handling of data during processing.

The directory uses the SpillCallBackPathsFinder class to keep track of spilled files and their positions. It also utilizes the IntermediateEncryptedStream class to handle encrypted intermediate files.

The security directory follows the Singleton pattern, ensuring that there is only one instance of the SpillCallBackPathsFinder class throughout the system. This pattern helps maintain a consistent and centralized approach to managing spilled files.

The directory also collaborates with the CryptoUtils class to perform encryption and decryption operations. This collaboration ensures that the security-related functionality is seamlessly integrated into the overall architecture.

In summary, the security directory plays a crucial role in managing security-related functionality within the Hadoop MapReduce architecture. It collaborates with other components to ensure the secure handling of data during processing, and follows established architectural patterns to maintain a well-structured and maintainable codebase.
